content:
21 min read
Just now

--

1. What is Angular?
“Angular is a front-end framework developed by Google that is used to build modern web applications, especially Single Page Applications (SPAs).
I like to think of Angular as a complete toolkit. It already provides features like routing, forms, API integration, dependency injection, and state management support, so we don’t have to depend on many external libraries.
Angular uses TypeScript, which helps us catch errors during development and makes the code more organized. Because of its structured architecture, Angular is widely used for large enterprise applications like banking systems, healthcare portals, CRM, and ERP software.”
Real-Time Example
“Suppose I’m building an e-commerce website like Amazon.
Instead of writing the entire application on one page, I’ll divide it into small components like:

• Header

• Product List

• Product Card

• Shopping Cart

• Footer
Each component handles its own responsibility. If I need to change the cart, I only modify the Cart component without affecting the other parts of the application.”
Benefits

• Easy to maintain

• Reusable components

• Faster development

• Better performance

• Suitable for large applications
Interview Closing Line
“So, in simple words, Angular helps us build scalable, maintainable, and high-performance web applications.”

2. Difference Between AngularJS and Angular
Interview Answer
“AngularJS is the older version released around 2010, while Angular is a completely rewritten framework released in 2016.
AngularJS was built using JavaScript and followed the MVC architecture. Angular uses TypeScript and follows a component-based architecture.
One major improvement is performance. Angular supports Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation, lazy loading, and better change detection, making applications much faster.
Angular also provides better tooling, improved security, and easier maintenance compared to AngularJS.”
Real-Time Example
“If a company has a banking application with hundreds of screens, maintaining it in AngularJS becomes difficult because everything is tightly coupled.
With Angular, the application is divided into independent components, so teams can work on different modules without affecting each other.”
Short Difference
AngularJSAngularJavaScriptTypeScriptMVCComponent-BasedSlowerFasterControllersComponentsLimited Mobile SupportMobile FriendlyLess ScalableHighly Scalable
Interview Closing Line
“So if I were starting a new project today, I would definitely choose Angular because it’s faster, more structured, and much easier to maintain.”

3. What are the Features of Angular?
Interview Answer
“Angular provides many built-in features that make application development easier.
Some of the important features are:
Component-Based Architecture — We divide the application into small reusable components.
TypeScript — It provides strong typing, better code quality, and catches errors during development.
Dependency Injection — Angular automatically provides required services instead of creating objects manually.
Routing — We can navigate between pages without refreshing the browser.
Forms — Angular supports both Template-driven and Reactive Forms.
HTTP Client — It makes API calls easy.
Pipes — Used to format data, like dates, currency, or uppercase text.
Directives — Used to dynamically change the HTML.
Lazy Loading — Loads modules only when needed, improving application performance.”
Real-Time Example
“In my project, when the user logs in, Angular loads only the Login module initially.
The Dashboard module is loaded only after successful login using Lazy Loading. This reduces the initial loading time and improves performance.”
Interview Closing Line
“These built-in features make Angular a complete framework for building enterprise-level applications.”

4. Explain Angular A
“Angular follows a component-based architecture.
The application starts with the App Module, which loads the root component. From there, the application is divided into multiple components.
Each component is responsible for a specific part of the UI and uses services whenever it needs business logic or API calls.
Angular provides Dependency Injection to automatically inject those services into components.
Templates display the data, directives control the HTML behavior, pipes format the data, and routing helps us move between pages.”
Real-Time Example
“Let’s say I’m building an Employee Management System.
When the user opens the Employee page:

• The Employee Component is displayed.

• It calls the Employee Service.

• The service makes an API request to the backend.

• The backend returns employee data.

• The component receives the data and displays it on the screen.”
Flow
User Clicks Employee↓Employee Component↓Employee Service↓Backend API↓Database↓Response↓Component Displays DataInterview Closing Line
“So, Angular architecture separates UI, business logic, and data access, making applications clean, scalable, and easy to maintain.”

5. What are Components in Angular?
Interview Answer
“A component is the basic building block of an Angular application.
Every part of the screen that the user sees is usually a component. For example, the header, sidebar, product list, footer, and login form are all separate components.
Each component contains three things:

• TypeScript file for business logic

• HTML file for the UI

• CSS file for styling
This separation keeps the code organized and easy to manage.”
Real-Time Example
“Suppose I’m building an online shopping website.
I’ll create separate components like:
Home Page├── Header
├── Search Bar
├── Category List
├── Product List
├── Shopping Cart
└── FooterIf tomorrow the client asks me to change the cart design, I only need to update the Cart component. The rest of the application remains unchanged.”
Why Do We Use Components?

• Code can be reused in multiple places.

• Easy to maintain.

• Multiple developers can work on different components at the same time.

• Easier to test and debug.

• Makes large applications more organized.
Interview Closing Line
“So, components help us break a large application into small, reusable, and independent parts, making development and maintenance much easier.”
1. What is a Module in Angular?
Interview Answer
“A module is a way to organize related components, directives, pipes, and services into a single unit.
Think of it like a folder that groups similar functionalities together. Instead of keeping everything in one place, we divide the application into modules to make it easier to manage and scale.
Every Angular application has at least one root module called AppModule. In large applications, we also create feature modules like EmployeeModule, AdminModule, UserModule, etc.”

Real-Time Example
“In my project, we had different modules such as:

• Login Module

• Dashboard Module

• Employee Module

• Reports Module
Each module contained its own components, services, and routing. This helped different developers work independently without affecting other modules.”

Benefits

• Better code organization

• Easy maintenance

• Supports Lazy Loading

• Faster application loading

• Suitable for large applications

Interview Closing Line
“So, a module helps us organize the application into logical sections, making it easier to develop and maintain.”

2. Difference Between Component and Module
Interview Answer
“A component is responsible for displaying a specific part of the user interface, whereas a module is responsible for organizing multiple related components and other Angular features together.”

Simple Example
Suppose you’re building an Employee Management System.
Employee Module

• Employee List Component

• Employee Details Component

• Employee Form Component

• Employee Service
Here, the module groups everything related to employees, while each component handles a specific screen or functionality.

Difference Table
ComponentModuleBuilds part of the UIGroups related featuresContains HTML, CSS, TypeScriptContains components, services, directives, pipesSmall unitLarger organizational unitReusable UIOrganizes the applicationExample: Header ComponentExample: Employee Module

Interview Closing Line
“In simple words, a component builds the UI, while a module organizes multiple related components and features.”

3. What is SPA (Single Page Application)?
Interview Answer
“SPA stands for Single Page Application.
In a Single Page Application, the browser loads the application only once. After that, when users navigate between pages, Angular updates only the required content instead of reloading the entire page.
This makes the application much faster and provides a smoother user experience.”

Real-Time Example
“When you use applications like Gmail or Facebook, clicking different sections doesn’t refresh the whole page. Only the content changes.
Angular applications work the same way using routing.”

Traditional Website vs SPA
Traditional Website
User clicks About↓Entire page reloads↓New page loadsSingle Page Application
User clicks About↓Angular changes only the content↓No page refreshAdvantages

• Faster navigation

• Better user experience

• Less server load

• Only required data is loaded

• Feels like a desktop application

Interview Closing Line
“So, SPA improves performance because the page loads only once, and Angular updates only the necessary content whenever users navigate.”

4. What is Angular CLI?
Interview Answer
“Angular CLI stands for Command Line Interface.
It is an official tool provided by Angular that helps developers create, build, run, test, and generate Angular applications using simple commands.
Instead of creating files manually, Angular CLI automatically generates components, services, modules, pipes, and many other files.”

Common Commands
Create a new project
ng new employee-managementRun the application
ng serveGenerate a component
ng generate component employeeGenerate a service
ng generate service employeeBuild the application
ng buildRun tests
ng testReal-Time Example
“In my project, whenever I needed a new component, I used Angular CLI instead of creating files manually.
For example:
ng g c employee-listAngular automatically created all the required files and updated the module.”

Benefits

• Saves development time

• Reduces manual work

• Generates standard project structure

• Minimizes errors

• Easy build and deployment

Interview Closing Line
“So, Angular CLI is a productivity tool that automates common development tasks and follows Angular best practices.”

5. Explain Angular Project Structure
Interview Answer
“When we create an Angular project using Angular CLI, it generates a predefined folder structure. Each folder has a specific responsibility, making the application well-organized.”

Important Folders
src/app/assets/environments/node_modules/angular.jsonpackage.jsonapp/
“This is the heart of the application.
It contains components, services, modules, routing, and business logic.”

assets/
“Stores static files like images, icons, fonts, and JSON files.”

environments/
“Contains environment-specific configuration files such as development and production API URLs.”

node_modules/
“Contains all third-party packages installed through npm.”

package.json
“Contains project information, dependencies, and npm scripts.”

angular.json
“Contains Angular project configuration like build settings, assets, and styles.”

Real-Time Example
“In my project:

• Components were inside the app folder.

• Images were stored in assets.

• Development and production API URLs were managed in environment files.

• Third-party libraries were listed in package.json.”

Interview Closing Line
“The Angular project structure keeps the application organized, making it easier for teams to develop and maintain large projects.”

6. Components & Templates
What is a Template?
Interview Answer
“A template is the HTML view of a component. It defines what the user sees on the screen.
The component contains the business logic
